steps.title: Read the vertex from the original graph
steps.content: The parabola shown opens upward with its vertex at the lowest point. From the graph, the vertex is located at (2, −2).

steps.title: Translate the vertex up 4 units
steps.content: Translating up $4$ units means adding $4$ to every $y$-coordinate while keeping each $x$-coordinate the same. The vertex moves from $(2,-2)$ to $$x=2,\qquad y=-2+4=2.$$ The new vertex is $(2,2)$, which matches the correct answer, A.
